steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question testing a paradox. Water is “critical to most reactions that enable life,” yet the text says the presence of water likely ______ biomolecule formation during Earth’s earliest oceans. The word “although” signals the blank must be the opposite of helpful — something that hindered or impeded.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — reflected: “reflected” means showed or indicated. Water reflecting biomolecule formation is neither a negative nor a paradox — it does not complete the contrast the sentence requires. ✗
  • Choice B — hampered: “hampered” means hindered or impeded. Water being life-critical yet impeding biomolecule formation is precisely the paradox the study addresses. ✓
  • Choice C — imitated: “imitated” means copied or mimicked. There is no meaningful sense in which the presence of water could copy the formation of biomolecules, and this creates no paradox. ✗
  • Choice D — exploited: “exploited” means took advantage of. Water exploiting a biomolecular process is conceptually incoherent in this context and introduces no logical paradox. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
